content.question: Can community mobilization (as observed in Erdőkürt and Polish cases) be scaled or institutionalized as a mechanism for school retention in post-2013 Hungary, or does effectiveness depend on ad-hoc grassroots action unamenable to policy design?
content.reduction_path: Document the organizational structure, funding, and outcomes of successful school-retention mobilizations in Hungary and Poland post-2000, identify common success factors, and pilot a structured 'community-led school review' process in 5–10 villages, with support from regional KLIK offices and municipal administrations, tracking retention decisions and stakeholder participation over 2–3 years
